provenance.source_corpus: Lichen matted seedbeds inhibit while moss dominated seedbeds facilitate black spruce (Picea mariana) seedling regeneration in post-fire boreal forest

source_spans.text: Lichen matted seedbeds inhibit while moss dominated seedbeds facilitate black spruce (Picea mariana) seedling regeneration in post-fire boreal forest

Lichen matted seedbeds inhibit while moss dominated seedbeds facilitate black spruce (Picea mariana) seedling regeneration in post-fire boreal forest
HPLC analysis of C. cristatella indicated the presence of usnic acid, a common germination inhibiting allelochemical.
HPLC analysis of C. cristatella indicated the presence of usnic acid, a common germination inhibiting allelochemical.
HPLC analysis of C. cristatella indicated the presence of usnic acid, a common germination inhibiting allelochemical.
Soil moisture was higher in moss seedbed than lichen seedbed at 1 cm depth.

Lichen seedbeds experienced a greater fluctuation of diurnal temperature than moss seedbeds... At 1 cm depth diurnal temperature ranges in lichen and moss seedbeds were respectively 35– 40 °C and 20 to 25 °C.
mat manipulation treatments (mat mixing and mat removal) eliminated the favourable microsite condition
